messages.content: Build a pure Decider for an Enrolment aggregate in TypeScript. Support register, confirm, and drop commands. Throw a typed DomainError if a confirmed enrolment is dropped after the drop date.
Explain briefly how a Decider keeps a Course aggregate consistent, then implement one with open, schedule, and close transitions in TypeScript using strict types.

Build an Assignment Decider in TypeScript with assign, submit, and grade commands. A submission can only be graded after it is submitted; use a discriminated union for state and exhaustive never defaults.

Write a Submission Decider that tracks a learner turning in work. Commands: start, attach, turnIn, retract. Constraints: strict types, no any, exhaustive never defaults, cannot turn in without an attachment.
What's the idiomatic way to model a Discussion thread as a Decider in TypeScript? Show a minimal working example with open, post, and archive.
Build a Cohort Decider in TypeScript. A cohort can be formed, have learners admitted up to a capacity, and then be sealed. Admitting past capacity must throw a typed error.
How do I project a Grade event stream into a per-learner gradebook view in TypeScript? Show a minimal project function that ignores events outside its interest.
Build a LectureAttendance projection in TypeScript that folds attendance events into a per-lecture headcount and a set of present learners.

Write a Syllabus projection that builds an ordered list of modules from SyllabusModuleAdded / SyllabusModuleRemoved / SyllabusReordered events. Constraints: immutable updates, ignore events for other syllabi, exhaustive never default.
Add a LabBooked / LabReleased projection to this Lab event stream so we can query which labs are currently in use. Keep the projection pure and immutable.
How do I snapshot an Enrolment aggregate with a versioned upgrade pipeline in TypeScript? Show the Snapshot type, two upgraders, and a loader.
Build a versioned Snapshot loader for a Course aggregate in TypeScript. Support v1 (id, title) → v2 (+capacity) → v3 (+status). Throw UpgradeError on unknown versions.
Write a Snapshot module for a Certificate aggregate. Constraints: Snapshot<S> tagged by version, upgraders run in a loop, and the loader returns Snapshot<CurrentCertificate> or throws.

Build an append-with-optimistic-concurrency helper for an Assignment stream. Use a local EventStore interface with expectedRevision: bigint | "no_stream" | "any" and throw a typed ConcurrencyConflict on mismatch.
What's the right way to retry a Submission write when the event store reports a concurrent revision conflict? Show a small bounded retry loop.
Refactor this imperative write path into a reusable CommandHandler-style appender for a Cohort stream using expectedRevision and a typed ConcurrencyConflict. Keep decide / evolve pure.
Build a CommandHandler that loads a Grade aggregate from the event store, runs decide, and appends the resulting events with expectedRevision. Keep the handler as orchestration only.
Explain how a CommandHandler keeps IO at the edges, then implement one for an Enrolment aggregate in TypeScript using Effect.gen for the event-store boundary.
How do I wire a Course aggregate's decide/evolve to a local EventStore interface so the whole handler is testable with an in-memory stub?
Implement a CommandHandler for a Certificate aggregate in TypeScript using Effect.gen. Constraints: pure decide/evolve, typed EventStore interface with expectedRevision, no any.
Build a checkpointed subscription runner that projects Lecture events into a read model. Include a local CheckpointStore interface and crash-safe ordering (apply then write).
Explain briefly how a checkpointed subscription resumes a Syllabus projection after a crash, then implement one in TypeScript using Effect.gen for the IO boundary.
How do I build a catch-up subscription that replays missed Submission events into a projection after downtime? Use a local CheckpointStore and a typed async iterable.
Build a generic aggregateStream helper that folds an AsyncIterable of events into aggregate state, then use it to rebuild a Course aggregate.
Show me the idiomatic `when(state, event)` reducer for an Assignment aggregate in TypeScript, with an exhaustive switch and a never default.
Write an aggregateStream helper that works for any aggregate, then apply it to a Lab aggregate. Constraints: two type parameters, no mutation of the input iterable, exhaustive evolve.
Add a `when` reducer and an `aggregate(events)` helper to this Prerequisite event union so downstream code can replay into state.
Build an upcast function that migrates old Enrolment events to the current shape. Old EnrolmentRegistered v1 has `studentId`; current v2 renames it to `learner`.
Fix this Grade upcaster so it converts v1 `mark: number` to v2 `score: number` and leaves v2 events unchanged. Keep the exhaustive never default.
Implement a Discussion read-model rebuild that wipes the projection store and re-projects from revision 0 in batches, with optional progress reporting and a resumable `from` option.
How do I rebuild a Cohort projection in chunks and persist progress so a crashed rebuild can resume? Show a minimal loop with typed local interfaces.

Build a minimal CQRS boundary for Enrolment in TypeScript: handleCommand writes via decide + appendToStream; queryView reads from a read store. The read model is eventually consistent with the event store.
Explain the CQRS split for a Course aggregate, then implement handleCommand and queryView for it in TypeScript.

Write a stateless process manager that reacts to Enrolment events and emits Cohort admission commands. Constraints: pure `react(event): Command | null`, return null for events it ignores, exhaustive never default, and a `project(events)` helper that folds the saga.

Add a process manager that reacts to SubmissionTurnedIn by emitting a RecordGrade command. Keep the saga pure, return null for unrelated events, and expose a `project(events)` helper that collects the commands.
How do I translate internal Grade domain events into a versioned IntegrationEvent for an outbox, returning null for events that don't cross the boundary?
Build a transactional append-with-outbox function for a Certificate stream. One call inserts outbox rows and appends events atomically; a separate worker publishes rows.

Build a Prerequisite Decider that guards against circular course dependencies. Commands: declare, require, remove. Throw a typed error if requiring a course would create a cycle against a provided adjacency map.
Fix this Lecture stream folder so the IO boundary uses Effect.gen while the evolve function stays pure. Keep aggregateStream generic.
Write a decide-with-domain-guard for an Assignment that cannot be graded if it was retracted by the learner. Use a typed AssignmentError and an exhaustive never default.
